title: Compatibility of the Fargues-Scholze and Gan-Takeda Local Langlands

thm:8.1.summary: Main compatibility: the Fargues–Scholze correspondence agrees with the classical Gan–Takeda (for $\mathrm{GSp}_4$) and Gan–Tantono (for the inner form $\mathrm{GU}_2(D)$) local Langlands correspondences — fully in the non-supercuspidal case, and for supercuspidal parameters when $L/\mathbb{Q}_p$ is unramified and $p>2$. The first nontrivial check pinning the abstract FS map to a known LLC.

thm:8.2.summary: Middle-degree concentration: for $\pi, \rho$ in the $L$-packet of a supercuspidal $\mathrm{GSp}_4$-parameter ($L/\mathbb{Q}_p$ unramified, $p>2$), the cohomology complexes $R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\pi]$ and $R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\rho]$ are concentrated in middle degree — a geometric realization of the packet.

thm:1.2.summary: Recovers (via Harris–Taylor / Boyer) compatibility of FS with the Jacquet–Langlands transfer for supercuspidals of $\mathrm{GL}_n$ and the division algebra $D^*_{1/n}$, used as a known-case input and benchmark.

thm:2.1.summary: Gan–Takeda local Langlands for $\mathrm{GSp}_4$: a surjective finite-to-one map $\mathrm{LLC}_G: \Pi(\mathrm{GSp}_4) \to \Phi(\mathrm{GSp}_4)$ with the expected packet properties — the classical correspondence FS is checked against.

thm:2.2.summary: Gan–Tantono local Langlands for the inner form $\mathrm{GU}_2(D)$: the analogous surjective finite-to-one parametrization on the non-quasi-split side.

lem:3.1.summary: Imported from FS (Prop VII.7.1): equivalence $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(B\underline{J_b(\mathbb{Q}_p)}) \simeq D(J_b(\mathbb{Q}_p))$ between sheaves on the classifying stack and smooth representations — the dictionary letting cohomology of moduli of shtukas be read as representations.

thm:3.2.summary: Imported from FS (Thm I.7.2 / Prop IX.2.1): the Hecke operators $T_W$ on $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ and their finiteness properties — the geometric Hecke action used throughout.

thm:3.4.summary: Imported from FS (Cor I.7.3): the cohomology $R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\rho]$ is valued in finite-length smooth admissible $G(\mathbb{Q}_p)$-reps with $W_E$-action, concentrated in degrees $0 \leq i \leq 2d$.

thm:3.5.summary: Imported from FS: to each Schur-irreducible $A$ in $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ is attached a unique semisimple $L$-parameter $\phi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\pi$ — the Fargues–Scholze parametrization map itself.

thm:3.6.summary: Imported from FS (Thm I.9.6): properties of $\pi \mapsto \phi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\pi$ — compatibility with local class field theory for tori, with twists, contragredient, parabolic induction, and central characters. The list of constraints the compatibility proof exploits.

thm:3.7.summary: Imported from FS (Cor X.I.3): the spectral action of $\mathrm{Perf}(Z^1(W_{\mathbb{Q}_p},\widehat{G})/\widehat{G})$ on $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)^\omega$, the categorical incarnation of the moduli of $L$-parameters acting on cohomology.

prop:3.14.summary: Imported from FS (§IX.7.1): the excursion algebra acts on $D_{\mathrm{lis}}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ commuting with Hecke operators and compatibly with restriction to HN-strata $\mathrm{Bun}_G^b$ — the mechanism transferring the parameter between strata.

thm:3.13.summary: Kaletha–Weinstein input: for a discrete parameter $\phi$, structure of the $L$-packets $\Pi_\phi(G)$, $\Pi_\phi(J)$ and the $S_\phi$-action used to index packet members.

thm:3.17.summary: Hansen's basic-uniformization criterion: conditions under which moduli of shtukas $\mathrm{Sht}(G,b,\mu)$ realize the local correspondence, used to access $R\Gamma_c$ geometrically.

thm:3.18.summary: Hansen's duality: for $\rho$ with supercuspidal FS parameter, $R\mathcal{H}om(R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\rho], \overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell) \simeq R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\rho^*][2d](d)$ as $W_E$-modules — the Poincaré-duality symmetry forcing middle-degree concentration.

prop:4.1.summary: Basic uniformization map $\Theta$: relates the cohomology of moduli of shtukas $R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)$ (with automorphic coefficients) to the cohomology of the Shimura variety $\mathcal{S}(\mathbf{G},X)$ — the bridge between the local geometric and global automorphic pictures.

thm:4.2.summary: Shen's theorem: abelian-type Shimura data with $G$ unramified at $p>2$ satisfy basic uniformization at $p$ — supplies the geometric hypothesis Prop 4.1 needs.

thm:4.5.summary: Boyer's-trick chart: the Hodge–Tate period morphism $h^{\leftarrow,b}_G$ presents the relevant local Shimura variety as a quotient, making the uniformization map computable.

thm:5.2.summary: Existence of strong transfers: via the simple (and $\sigma$-twisted) trace formula, any suitable local $\pi$ globalizes to a cuspidal automorphic $\tau$ on $\mathrm{GSp}_4/F$ with prescribed local components at the bad places — the engine that turns a local statement into an accessible global one.

prop:5.4.summary: Globalization conditions: with $|S_{\mathrm{st}}| \geq 3$, cohomological regular-weight cuspidal $\pi$ unramified outside $S$ and Steinberg-type at chosen places exist — the input set making Thm 5.2 applicable.

thm:6.1.summary: Sorensen-type Galois representations: a globally generic $\tau$ with $S_{\mathrm{st}}$ nonempty has an associated $\mathrm{GSp}_4$-valued Galois representation $\rho_\tau$ in the cohomology of the Shimura variety — the global Galois input matched against FS parameters.

prop:6.2.summary: Local–global Frobenius matching: at almost all good places, traces of $\rho_{\mathrm{shim}}$ agree with the Hecke/Satake data, pinning the global Galois representation to the automorphic side.

prop:7.2.summary: Eigenspace localization: for $\rho$ with supercuspidal Gan–Tantono parameter, globalizing to $\Pi'$ and localizing the Shimura-variety cohomology at the corresponding Hecke maximal ideal $\mathfrak{m}$ identifies the local FS contribution inside global cohomology.

prop:7.4.summary: The Key Proposition: the supercuspidal-Bernstein direct summand of $\bigoplus_{\rho'} R\Gamma_c(G,b,\mu)[\rho']$ over a supercuspidal $L$-packet has the predicted structure (charged by the right $L$-parameter, multiplicity-one). The technical heart from which both main theorems follow.

edges.note: The supercuspidal case of compatibility is read off the Key Proposition's identification of the supercuspidal summand.
Compatibility is forced by matching against the FS correspondence's known properties (LCFT for tori, twists, parabolic induction) from FS Thm I.9.6.
The target of the comparison is the Gan–Takeda LLC for $\mathrm{GSp}_4$.
...and the Gan–Tantono LLC for the inner form $\mathrm{GU}_2(D)$.
The non-supercuspidal case uses the Kaletha–Weinstein packet structure to reduce to known parametrizations.
Middle-degree concentration uses the Key Proposition's structure of the supercuspidal summand.
Concentration in middle degree follows from Hansen's Poincaré-duality symmetry of $R\Gamma_c[\rho]$.
The summand structure is established after eigenspace-localizing global cohomology at the Hecke maximal ideal (Prop 7.2).
Transfer of the parameter to the basic stratum uses excursion–Hecke compatibility and HN-strata compatibility (FS §IX.7.1).
The spectral action (FS Cor X.I.3) supplies the $L$-parameter grading on the cohomology summand.
Localization requires a globalization $\Pi'$ with controlled ramification, produced by the strong-transfer theorem.
Identifying the local contribution inside global cohomology uses the basic-uniformization map $\Theta$.
The Galois representation $\rho_\tau$ realizes the expected parameter in the localized cohomology.
Frobenius-trace matching pins the localized cohomology to the correct Hecke eigensystem.
Strong transfer is applied to the cohomological, Steinberg-type globalizations supplied by Prop 5.4.
The uniformization map exists because abelian-type data with $G$ unramified satisfy basic uniformization (Shen).
The Hodge–Tate period chart makes the uniformization map explicit.
The Frobenius-trace identities are statements about the Galois representation $\rho_\tau$ of Thm 6.1.
The FS parametrization is built from the Hecke operators (both imported from FS).

classical_llc.description: The known correspondences FS is benchmarked against.

fs_machinery.description: The Fargues–Scholze apparatus imported wholesale: representation dictionary, Hecke action, the parametrization, its properties, the spectral action, and excursion–Hecke compatibility.

geometric_realization.description: Basic uniformization and duality relating moduli of shtukas to Shimura-variety cohomology.

global_input.description: Trace-formula globalization and the resulting Galois representations.

key_proposition.description: Eigenspace localization yielding the supercuspidal-summand structure, from which both main theorems follow.

main_results.description: Compatibility with Gan–Takeda/Gan–Tantono and middle-degree concentration.

FS.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
FS.role: the apparatus imported in §3: the representation dictionary (VII.7.1), Hecke operators (I.7.2), cohomology finiteness (I.7.3), the parametrization and its properties (I.9.6), the spectral action (X.I.3), and excursion–Hecke compatibility (IX.7.1)

GT1.title: Gan–Takeda, The local Langlands conjecture for GSp(4)

GT2.title: Gan–Tantono, The local Langlands conjecture for GU(2,2) / inner forms
GT2.role: the classical LLC for the inner form $\mathrm{GU}_2(D)$ (Thm 2.2)

Han.title: Hansen, basic uniformization and duality for moduli of shtukas
Han.role: geometric realization and Poincaré duality (Thm 3.17, Thm 3.18)

She.title: Shen, basic uniformization of abelian-type Shimura varieties

KW.title: Kaletha–Weinstein, on L-packets and the cohomology of moduli of shtukas

So.title: Sorensen / source for GSp_4 Galois representations (Theorem A)

HT.title: Harris–Taylor, The geometry and cohomology of some simple Shimura varieties

Boy.title: Boyer, on the cohomology of Lubin–Tate / Drinfeld towers
